focus: RQ4: doctor manifest-backed subaction topology and schema-aware executable-edge traversal
noveltyJustification: 4 of 5 findings were fully new contract or algorithm details and 1 formalized the known comment-cycle defect.
ruledOut: Comment stripping followed by raw regex scanning

title: Doctor is a manifest-backed subaction router
summary: Routing source is a subtype discriminator beneath the existing subaction-router topology, not a fifth topology.

title: Manifest sections require owner-scoped traversal
summary: Only routes[] belongs to /doctor:speckit; mcp_subroutes[] and standalone[] are owned by companion commands.

title: Raw-text target extraction creates false cycles
summary: The current global path regex scans comments and the cycle check only tests immediate back-edges.

title: Executable edges need a schema registry
summary: Direct, subaction, and workflow dispatch fields emit typed edges; comments and non-executable strings emit none.

title: Cycle checks require complete directed traversal
summary: Strongly connected components catch self-loops and cycles longer than the current two-node reciprocal check.

statement: YAML comments, documentation paths, globs, presentation sources, and foreign-owner manifest sections never create executable edges.
Every genuine direct, inline-subaction, manifest-subaction, or workflow-dispatch cycle fails with a typed ordered edge path.

acceptanceCriterion: The four-class census is unchanged while doctor subaction routers gain deterministic manifest or inline-table routing-source subtypes.
Owner-scoped traversal emits /doctor:speckit edges only from routes[*].yaml; comments and foreign-owner sections emit none.
Typed complete-graph cycle traversal ignores comment-only paths and fails executable cycles of any length.
Negative comment fixture passes and direct, inline-subaction, manifest-subaction, and workflow-cycle fixtures fail.
Owner and dispatch-role schema gates fail closed before asset load while existing A-K assertions remain green.
